page: OVHcloud a100-180 is a dedicated GPU instance within the Cloud GPU family, running on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 CPU architecture with 15 dedicated vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 3.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 180.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 12.0 GB per vCPU.

The primary accelerator is a single NVIDIA A100 GPU from the Ampere architecture family, featuring 80 GB of dedicated VRAM. Local storage consists of a 300 GB NVMe SSD, which is bundled directly with the instance. Network connectivity is supported by an 8 Gbps baseline bandwidth, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

This hardware profile is designed for workloads requiring massive parallel processing capabilities and substantial memory capacity, such as large language model inference, deep learning training, and complex data science tasks. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio and dedicated GPU resources offer a specialized configuration for memory-intensive acceleration tasks, balancing compute resources with high-capacity VRAM and fast local NVMe storage.
description: The OVHcloud a100-180 is a dedicated GPU instance built on the KVM hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It features 15 dedicated vCPUs running at 3.0 GHz, paired with 180.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high ratio of 12.0 GB of RAM per core. Acceleration is driven by a single NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPU with 80 GB of VRAM. For local storage, the instance includes a bundled 300 GB NVMe SSD. Network performance is supported by an 8 Gbps baseline bandwidth, and the server comes with one complimentary public IPv4 address. This configuration is optimized for workloads that demand significant GPU memory and parallel processing power, such as deep learning, large-scale AI model execution, and data-intensive scientific computing.
